thesis: Pan American Silver is the world's largest primary silver producer, offering unmatched leverage to silver price moves. The Juanicipio JV has transformed the cost curve, lowering AISC to $14.50–$16/oz and driving record Q4 2025 results. Silver faces a structural supply deficit driven by solar, EV, and AI demand, while investment demand provides a monetary hedge kicker. Escobal restart and further Juanicipio ramp are key upside catalysts. Geopolitical and operational risks warrant a SPECULATIVE BUY rating.

drivers: Silver rallies to $50+/oz on widening supply deficit and investment demand
Juanicipio reaches full design capacity, further lowering AISC
Escobal permit resolution adds $8–10/share in NAV
Critical minerals designation unlocks subsidies and strategic offtake flows
Gold remains elevated, supporting by-product revenue

assumptions: Operational execution continues with modest volume growth
Margins compress slightly from current highs but remain healthy
No Escobal restart in the 12–18 month horizon

risks: Global recession crushes industrial silver demand; spot falls to $25/oz
Cost inflation (labor, energy, consumables) erodes margins
Escobal remains suspended indefinitely, removing growth option
Political risk premium rises in Bolivia, Argentina, or Guatemala
Stronger USD and higher real rates suppress precious metals

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 production report — watch Juanicipio volumes and AISC trends
Escobal legal/permit updates — any positive ruling is transformative
Q3 2026 earnings — guidance for 2026 silver production and cost outlook
Fed policy meetings — rate-cut trajectory affects precious metals sentiment

key_risks: Commodity price volatility — silver is more volatile than gold; 20% spot drop crushes margins
Geopolitical risk — Bolivia, Argentina, Guatemala face regulatory and social-license risks
Escobal uncertainty — suspended since 2017 with no clear restart timeline
Cost inflation — labor, energy, and consumable costs rising across the mining sector
Environmental incidents — tailings or water disputes can halt operations
FX risk — stronger local currencies vs. USD raise operating costs
